title: Who will benefit most from SpaceX IPO? Mostly Elon — and a few from his inner circle
description: Elon Musk has the largest stake in SpaceX by billions of shares. The other biggest shareholders also have longstanding and deep ties to Musk.

title: Waymo halts freeway rides after robotaxis struggle in construction zones
description: The service suspension comes as Waymo has also paused operations in Atlanta and San Antonio after its robotaxis drove into flooded streets.

title: Forget ‘TechnoKing’: Elon Musk will really be king at SpaceX
description: Musk, who will have more than 50% of the voting power, will have a monarchical grip over the publicly-traded version of SpaceX — control that goes far beyond what other tech founders enjoy.

title: The SpaceX IPO Filing Looks Nothing Like Those Of The Elite Group Of Tech Giants It’s Hoping To Join
description: SpaceX filed its public IPO prospectus Wednesday, highlighting many amazing things that it has accomplished in its reach for a trillion-dollar-plus valuation. title: Spotify launches an ElevenLabs-powered audiobook creation tool
description: Spotify is releasing new audiobook plans later this year

title: Waymo pauses Atlanta service as its robotaxis keep driving into floods
description: Waymo's robotaxi service is now suspended in both Atlanta and San Antonio, as the company works to stop its vehicles from driving into flooded roads.

title: Humanoid partners with Bosch, Schaeffler to scale robot production
description:
Humanoid is working with Bosch to make and distribute its HMND robot in Europe after also partnering with Siemens and Schaeffler.
The post Humanoid partners with Bosch, Schaeffler to scale robot production appeared first on The Robot Report.

title: Lexroom CEO: Anthropic won’t kill vertical AI companies
SpaceX S-1 reveals Tesla’s Terafab deal is far from done
description:
SpaceX filed its S-1 with the SEC today ahead of its blockbuster IPO, and buried in the 308-page document is a sobering reality check on the Tesla (TSLA) collaborations that Elon Musk has been hyping for months.

title: SpaceX Listed Grok’s ‘Spicy’ Mode as a Risk in Its IPO Filing
description: The rocket company has set aside more than $500 million for potential litigation losses, in part to account for complaints alleging that Grok created sexualized images.

title: SpaceX Is Spending $2.8 Billion to Buy Gas Turbines for Its AI Data Centers
description: The investment comes as Elon Musk’s AI unit faces complaints about the carbon-emitting units and looks to become a big player in cloud computing.

title: SpaceX IPO Filing Reveals Anthropic Is Paying $15 Billion a Year to Access Its Data Centers
description: The long-awaited documents SpaceX filed with US regulators Wednesday included details about a lucrative deal to lend GPUs to a major AI rival.

title: AI model "capability overhang" biggest challenge facing European businesses, says OpenAI revenue chief

title: Green steel startup Boston Metal is doubling down on critical metals
description: The startup Boston Metal has raised a $75 million funding round to produce critical metals, MIT Technology Review can exclusively report.   The company has been known largely for its efforts to clean up steel production, an industry that’s responsible for about 8% of global greenhous

title: Einride L4 autonomous electric semi truck gets real – in Ohio
description:
After a successful pilot in Europe, Swedish AI logistics firm Einride is deploying its SAE Level 4 autonomous electric semi trucks in the US, connecting EASE Logistics warehouses in Marysville, Ohio as part of the DriveOhio Truck Automation Corridor Project.



 more…

title: Singapore partners OpenAI in $234M push to expand national AI ecosystem
description: Singapore’s Ministry of Digital Development and Information (MDDI) and OpenAI will launch “OpenAI for Singapore,” a joint initiative aimed at strengthening the country’s position as a hub for applied AI innovation.

title: NATO eastern deterrence strategy takes shape around ‘autonomous zone’
description: “What we see in Ukraine, no vehicle movements are in the gray zone at all,” one Latvian commander said. “Any movement is destroyed.”

title: Dunia Innovations unveils €280M Berlin GigaLab to industrialise AI-driven materials discovery
description: Dunia Innovations today announced plans for Berlin GigaLab: a 6,000 m², €280 million facility designed to discover and develop advanced materials at industrial scale. Siemens, ABB Robotics, NVIDIA, AW...

title: Tesla’s Former AI Lead Goes To Anthropic
description: Andrej Karpathy was Tesla’s AI lead well before AI was everywhere all the time. At the very early part of the AI hype cycle, he was the only AI guy many people knew. He led Tesla’s efforts toward Full Self Driving (FSD) for years, and it’s still not exactly clear   ... [continued]

What US chipmaker Cerebras’s IPO could mean for startups like Fractile
When Refusal Doesn’t Matter: Operation Epic Fury and the Erosion of Host Nation Consent
description: When the U.S. military launched its war against Iran in Feb. 2026, it did not just dismantle Iranian military capabilities. It shattered the illusion that the United States would consult with its closest allies and that an ally’s refusal to grant base access can stop an American war in motion.

title: X-energy gets federal environmental approval for Texas nuclear reactors

description:
In the Electrek Podcast, we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss updates to Tesla Robotaxi, Rivian R2 configurator going live, China taking over EU factories, and more.

title: Rivian opens R2 configurator: here are all the options and pricing
description:
Rivian has opened the online configurator for the R2, its critical mid-size electric SUV, giving reservation holders their first chance to spec out the vehicle ahead of deliveries. The Performance trim starts at $57,990 — just under Tesla’s Model Y Performance at $58,880.

title: Uber turns on Waymo as it pours $10B+ into owning robotaxi alternatives
description:
Uber is publicly trashing its robotaxi partner Waymo while simultaneously investing more than $10 billion to build its own autonomous vehicle fleet with Rivian, Lucid, and Nuro.

Waymo expands robotaxi coverage more than 20% — larger than Rhode Island
description:
Waymo announced a major expansion of its autonomous robotaxi service area, growing to over 1,400 square miles across 11 US cities. That’s an estimated 27% increase from its previous coverage and more territory than the entire state of Rhode Island.

description: BMS is the latest large pharma company to make a major bet on AI, announcing a sweeping agreement with Anthropic to deploy its AI tool Claude as the “shared intelligence platform” across the drugmaker’s global operations.
